skill.name: antigravity-workflows
skill.description: Orchestrate Antigravity workflow playbooks for SaaS MVP delivery, web security review, AI agent builds, and browser QA.
skill.summary: Guide multi-step Antigravity workflows from planning through validation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby shell execution and ordinary prose as weak cryptography. Manual review found no executable code, cryptographic implementation, network access, credential handling, or prompt injection attempt in the scanned files.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown References
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown inline code examples, workflow identifiers, and copy-paste prompt fences. They do not execute commands and contain no user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain Markdown content and fenced text prompts. No scripting language runtime, command execution API, or shell invocation is present.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are Prose False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are the skill description and a safety guardrail sentence. They do not define or call any cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive text only. There is no hash, cipher, key handling, or cryptographic code to misuse.

low_findings.title: Referenced Workflow Source Files Are Not Included
low_findings.description: The skill instructs users to read workflow metadata files, but the scanned file tree only includes SKILL.md and resources/implementation-playbook.md. This is an operational limitation, not a security issue.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file tree in the report omits the referenced workflow files. The impact is reliability and completeness rather than direct exploitation.

content.value_statement: Complex delivery work often needs several skills, checkpoints, and validation steps. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through structured Antigravity workflows for common product and QA tasks.
actual_capabilities: Routes product delivery, security review, AI agent, and browser QA requests to named workflows.
Guides users through step-by-step execution with artifacts for each phase.
Defines completion checks before moving to the next workflow step.
Provides copy-paste prompts for common Antigravity workflow requests.
Documents safety guardrails for destructive actions and authorized security testing.
limitations: It orchestrates other skills and does not implement specialized work by itself.
It depends on the availability of referenced skills in the local environment.
The scanned package does not include the workflow files named as source of truth.
It cannot guarantee success without project access, credentials, or required infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Plan and Ship a SaaS MVP
use_cases.description: Turn a product idea into a phased workflow with planning, build, testing, release, and risk tracking.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Web App Security Review
use_cases.description: Run an authorized web security review with clear scope, evidence collection, findings, and remediation follow-up.

use_cases.title: Stabilize Browser QA Workflows
use_cases.description: Guide browser automation work through test selection, execution, failure triage, and validation checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use antigravity-workflows to identify the best workflow for my goal: [describe the goal]. Ask me any required scope questions first.

prompt_templates.title: Run an MVP Delivery Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use antigravity-workflows to run the Ship a SaaS MVP workflow for this idea: [idea]. Produce artifacts and validation checks for each step.

prompt_templates.title: Execute a Security Audit Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use antigravity-workflows to run a web app security audit workflow for [application]. Confirm authorization, scope, test plan, findings format, and remediation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate a Multi-Skill Delivery Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use antigravity-workflows to coordinate planning, implementation, testing, and release for [project]. Recommend skills for each phase and require validation before continuing.

output_examples.input: Run a SaaS MVP workflow for a subscription analytics dashboard.
output: Recommended workflow: Ship a SaaS MVP.
Step 1 artifact: scope checklist with target users, core features, risks, and acceptance criteria.
Validation evidence: each phase must produce notes, test results, and remaining risks before release.

output_examples.input: Guide a security audit workflow for my staging web app.
output: Recommended workflow: Security Audit for a Web App.
Required checkpoint: confirm authorization, target scope, excluded systems, and testing windows.
Final artifact: findings summary with severity, evidence, impact, and remediation actions.

output_examples.input: Stabilize flaky browser tests before launch.
output: Recommended workflow: QA and Browser Automation.
Step sequence: identify failing paths, run targeted tests, triage failures, apply fixes, and rerun validation.
Completion criteria: stable test results and a list of unresolved risks.

best_practices: Confirm the objective, scope, and required artifacts before starting a workflow.
Validate each workflow step before moving to the next phase.
Document remaining risks, missing skills, and required user approvals clearly.
anti_patterns: Do not run destructive actions without explicit user approval.
Do not treat this skill as a replacement for specialized implementation or security skills.
Do not start security testing until authorization and scope are confirmed.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides an AI assistant through multi-step Antigravity workflows for delivery, security review, AI agent work, and browser QA.

faq.question: Does it run commands or scripts?
faq.answer: No. The scanned files contain workflow instructions and Markdown examples, not executable scripts.

faq.question: Can it build a complete product by itself?
faq.answer: No. It coordinates steps and recommends skills, but specialized implementation work still needs the right tools and project access.

faq.question: Is it safe for security audits?
faq.answer: Yes, when used with explicit authorization and clear scope. The playbook tells the agent to confirm authorization before security testing.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide the project goal, scope, available skills, environment access, and any required approvals or constraints.
